# Oracle Automation Next 10000

Generated: `2026-03-24T06:15:09.004Z`
Source: `oracle-cards.json`
Scope: black-border paper-card automation candidates ordered by seam priority. The queue exhausts the active graveyard / recursion seam first, then spills into the next highest-population seams.

Queued items: `10000`

## Queue Rules

- Ordered by family priority first, then by EDHREC rank, then by card name.
- Cards are deduped by `oracle_id`, so multi-print duplicates do not crowd out breadth.
- This queue is intentionally seam-priority driven: graveyard/recursion work comes first, then the generator rolls into broader high-population seams like token creation, direct damage, draw, sacrifice, counters, and search effects.
- Nim Deathmantle-style payment + return + attach recursion is explicitly kept in the queue even when the family is small.

## Family Summary

| Family | Category | Queued | Available | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Dynamic Graveyard Mana-Value Reanimation | Near-Term Graveyard Move-Zone | 1 | 1 | High-yield follow-up to the new static mana-value cap support; keep runtime-dependent caps explicit and conservative. |
| Counter-Bearing Graveyard Reanimation | Near-Term Graveyard Move-Zone | 3 | 3 | Extends the current with-counters support to more real-card variants, especially where extra legality or follow-up state matters. |

| Dies Triggers Returning The Card To Hand | Contextual Graveyard Recursion | 21 | 22 | Similar contextual binding seam, but with hand destination instead of battlefield. |
| Pay-To-Return Deathmantle-Style Recursion | Contextual Graveyard Recursion | 3 | 3 | Includes Nim Deathmantle-style payment + return + attachment bundles. |
| Entered Or Cast From Graveyard Checks | Graveyard Context / Conditional | 7 | 7 | Good follow-up once provenance is threaded more broadly across server and rules-engine paths. |
| Leave-Battlefield Exile Replacement Riders | Graveyard Context / Conditional | 4 | 4 | Important support glue for temporary recursion families such as unearth and similar reanimation effects. |
| Cast From Graveyard Permission Windows | Graveyard Permission / Replacement | 35 | 392 | Useful for later test runs because these create lots of visible automation gaps when not modeled cleanly. |
| Play From Graveyard Permission Windows | Graveyard Permission / Replacement | 25 | 27 | Covers lands and mixed play-permission text from graveyard. |
| Flashback Cards | Graveyard Permission / Replacement | 60 | 220 | Stable, populous graveyard-casting family to validate once permission windows and replacement text are tightened. |
| Unearth Cards | Graveyard Permission / Replacement | 40 | 60 | Pairs graveyard reanimation with the leave-battlefield exile replacement rider. |
| Escape And Similar Graveyard Alternate Costs | Graveyard Permission / Replacement | 55 | 108 | Includes escape-style casting/replay from graveyard with additional costs or modifiers. |
| Token Creation | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 1800 | 3264 | Large practical seam spanning straightforward token creation, tapped/token modifier variants, and delayed cleanup follow-ups. |
| Direct Damage And Damage-Based Follow-Ups | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 1600 | 2549 | Covers direct damage sentences, symmetric sweepers, and that-much/excess-damage follow-up families. |
| Draw And Draw-Scaling Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 1400 | 3626 | Dense seam for raw draw, target draw, each-player draw, and draw-scaling follow-up text. |
| Sacrifice And Sacrifice Follow-Up Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 1200 | 3677 | Useful after the graveyard slice because it overlaps strongly with death/reanimation bookkeeping and delayed cleanup. |

| Life Gain And Life Loss | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 1000 | 2341 | Dense practical seam for gain/lose life, target-player life swings, and equal-to/reference clauses. |
| Targeted Destroy Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 847 | 1160 | Broad removal seam that tends to be straightforward once target binding and rider text are preserved. |
| Library Search And Tutor Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 419 | 1004 | Large queue for search workflows, especially once resolution-queue driven player choice paths are tightened further. |
| Bounce And Return-To-Hand Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 0 | 715 | Captures both battlefield bounce and zone-return effects that tend to be good deterministic automation candidates. |
| Discard Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 0 | 2004 | Useful for queueing both deterministic discard counts and player-choice discard families for later resolution support. |
| Scry / Surveil / Topdeck Manipulation | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 0 | 995 | Good post-graveyard seam because it is common, visible in tests, and already has some queue-backed infrastructure on the server side. |
| Tap / Untap Effects | High-Population Follow-On Seams | 0 | 609 | Broad tactical seam with many deterministic single-target and each-target templates. |

### Sacrifice And Sacrifice Follow-Up Effects

5335. Evolving Wilds (EDHREC 16) - {T}, Sacrifice this land: Search your library for a basic land card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
5336. Myriad Landscape (EDHREC 24) - {2}, {T}, Sacrifice this land: Search your library for up to two basic land cards that share a land type, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
5337. Terramorphic Expanse (EDHREC 28) - {T}, Sacrifice this land: Search your library for a basic land card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
5338. An Offer You Can't Refuse (EDHREC 37) - (They're artifacts with "{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color.")
5339. Polluted Delta (EDHREC 38) - {T}, Pay 1 life, Sacrifice this land: Search your library for an Island or Swamp card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.

### +1/+1 Counter Placement And Counter Scaling

6535. Rhythm of the Wild (EDHREC 193) - (They enter with your choice of a +1/+1 counter or haste.)
6536. Hardened Scales (EDHREC 204) - If one or more +1/+1 counters would be put on a creature you control, that many plus one +1/+1 counters are put on it instead.
6537. Branching Evolution (EDHREC 382) - If one or more +1/+1 counters would be put on a creature you control, twice that many +1/+1 counters are put on that creature instead.
6538. Spark Double (EDHREC 392) - You may have this creature enter as a copy of a creature or planeswalker you control, except it enters with an additional +1/+1 counter on it if it's a creature, it enters with an additional loyalty counter on it if it's a planeswalker, and it isn't legendary.
6539. Forgotten Ancient (EDHREC 404) - Whenever a player casts a spell, you may put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.
6540. Kami of Whispered Hopes (EDHREC 426) - If one or more +1/+1 counters would be put on a permanent you control, that many plus one +1/+1 counters are put on that permanent instead.

### Library Search And Tutor Effects

9582. Path to Exile (EDHREC 15) - Its controller may search their library for a basic land card, put that card onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
9583. Cultivate (EDHREC 20) - Search your library for up to two basic land cards, reveal those cards, put one onto the battlefield tapped and the other into your hand, then shuffle.
9584. Farseek (EDHREC 22) - Search your library for a Plains, Island, Swamp, or Mountain card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
9585. Rampant Growth (EDHREC 26) - Search your library for a basic land card, put that card onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
9586. Nature's Lore (EDHREC 30) - Search your library for a Forest card, put that card onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9587. Kodama's Reach (EDHREC 35) - Search your library for up to two basic land cards, reveal those cards, put one onto the battlefield tapped and the other into your hand, then shuffle.
9588. Three Visits (EDHREC 44) - Search your library for a Forest card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9589. Demonic Tutor (EDHREC 58) - Search your library for a card, put that card into your hand, then shuffle.
9590. Enlightened Tutor (EDHREC 115) - Search your library for an artifact or enchantment card, reveal it, then shuffle and put that card on top.
9591. Mystical Tutor (EDHREC 139) - Search your library for an instant or sorcery card, reveal it, then shuffle and put that card on top.
9592. Worldly Tutor (EDHREC 164) - Search your library for a creature card, reveal it, then shuffle and put the card on top.
9593. Ash Barrens (EDHREC 188) - Basic landcycling {1} ({1}, Discard this card: Search your library for a basic land card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.)
9594. Gamble (EDHREC 231) - Search your library for a card, put that card into your hand, discard a card at random, then shuffle.
9595. Sword of the Animist (EDHREC 241) - Whenever equipped creature attacks, you may search your library for a basic land card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
9596. Skyshroud Claim (EDHREC 287) - Search your library for up to two Forest cards, put them onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9597. Entomb (EDHREC 316) - Search your library for a card, put that card into your graveyard, then shuffle.
9598. Finale of Devastation (EDHREC 327) - Search your library and/or graveyard for a creature card with mana value X or less and put it onto the battlefield.
9599. Buried Alive (EDHREC 381) - Search your library for up to three creature cards, put them into your graveyard, then shuffle.
9600. Land Tax (EDHREC 425) - At the beginning of your upkeep, if an opponent controls more lands than you, you may search your library for up to three basic land cards, reveal them, put them into your hand, then shuffle.
9601. Diabolic Tutor (EDHREC 444) - Search your library for a card, put that card into your hand, then shuffle.
9602. Fabricate (EDHREC 476) - Search your library for an artifact card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
9603. Wishclaw Talisman (EDHREC 503) - {1}, {T}, Remove a wish counter from this artifact: Search your library for a card, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
9604. Chord of Calling (EDHREC 543) - Search your library for a creature card with mana value X or less, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9605. Green Sun's Zenith (EDHREC 556) - Search your library for a green creature card with mana value X or less, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9606. Imperial Recruiter (EDHREC 597) - When this creature enters, search your library for a creature card with power 2 or less, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
9607. Knight of the White Orchid (EDHREC 602) - When this creature enters, if an opponent controls more lands than you, you may search your library for a Plains card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9608. Wood Elves (EDHREC 622) - When this creature enters, search your library for a Forest card, put that card onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
9609. Muddle the Mixture (EDHREC 643) - Transmute {1}{U}{U} ({1}{U}{U}, Discard this card: Search your library for a card with the same mana value as this card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.

## Notes
